title: Establish the Python CLI project skeleton and test harness

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting work on stronger behavior-first test coverage. Plan: expand Behave feature stories and step definitions to validate end-to-end demo behavior and reduce reliance on weak unit-only assertions.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented behavior-first test expansion with story-driven Gherkin. Added a new configured demo feature file, rewrote demo step definitions to remove no-op assertions, added scenarios for firehose-to-radar output, unknown group messaging, source-based grouping, and YAML-configured storage paths. Verified with USING RUNNER: behave.runner:Runner
Feature: Configured demo story # features/configured_demo_story.feature:1
  As an operator integrating Caducus
  I want behavior-driven config coverage for storage and analysis paths
  So that I can trust the CLI will honor YAML configuration in real usage
  Scenario: YAML configuration controls both event and analysis storage locations         # features/configured_demo_story.feature:6
    Given a local demo dataset file with HDFS-style log rows                              # features/steps/demo_steps.py:27
    And a Caducus data directory is configured                                            # features/steps/demo_steps.py:47
    And a Caducus YAML config file sets data_dir and biblicus reinforcement-memory paths  # features/steps/demo_steps.py:170
    When I run the Caducus demo run command with config for group_id "hdfs-demo:DataNode" # features/steps/demo_steps.py:78
    Then the command succeeds                                                             # features/steps/demo_steps.py:113
    And the configured Caducus data_dir contains canonical events                         # features/steps/demo_steps.py:192
    And the configured Biblicus analysis and vector directories exist                     # features/steps/demo_steps.py:201

Feature: Demo vertical slice # features/demo_vertical_slice.feature:1
  As an on-call operator
  I want a story-first demo that turns noisy logs into ranked radar blips
  So that I can quickly decide what to investigate first
  Feature: Demo vertical slice  # features/demo_vertical_slice.feature:1

  Scenario: Firehose logs become ranked radar output                                   # features/demo_vertical_slice.feature:10
    Given a local demo dataset file with HDFS-style log rows                           # features/steps/demo_steps.py:27
    And a Caducus data directory is configured                                         # features/steps/demo_steps.py:47
    When I run the Caducus demo run command for group_id "hdfs-demo:DataNode"          # features/steps/demo_steps.py:61
    Then the command succeeds                                                          # features/steps/demo_steps.py:113
    And Caducus writes one canonical event per log row                                 # features/steps/demo_steps.py:118
    And the radar output includes group "hdfs-demo:DataNode"                           # features/steps/demo_steps.py:135
    And the radar output includes ranked blips with weight, temporal, and member count # features/steps/demo_steps.py:141

  Scenario: Ingest-only mode stores canonical event shape                         # features/demo_vertical_slice.feature:17
    Given a local demo dataset file with HDFS-style log rows                      # features/steps/demo_steps.py:27
    And a Caducus data directory is configured                                    # features/steps/demo_steps.py:47
    When I run the Caducus demo ingest command with that input file               # features/steps/demo_steps.py:95
    Then the command succeeds                                                     # features/steps/demo_steps.py:113
    And each stored event has id, timestamp, source, group_id, text, and metadata # features/steps/demo_steps.py:128

  Scenario: Groups command reveals discoverable analysis targets    # features/demo_vertical_slice.feature:22
    Given a local demo dataset file with HDFS-style log rows        # features/steps/demo_steps.py:27
    And a Caducus data directory is configured                      # features/steps/demo_steps.py:47
    When I run the Caducus demo ingest command with that input file # features/steps/demo_steps.py:95
    And I run the Caducus groups command                            # features/steps/demo_steps.py:108
    Then the command succeeds                                       # features/steps/demo_steps.py:113
    And the CLI lists at least the group IDs from the ingested file # features/steps/demo_steps.py:149

  Scenario: Analyze uses previously stored events                                                       # features/demo_vertical_slice.feature:28
    Given a local demo dataset file with HDFS-style log rows                                            # features/steps/demo_steps.py:27
    And a Caducus data directory is configured                                                          # features/steps/demo_steps.py:47
    Given canonical event records already exist for group_id "hdfs-demo:DataNode" in the data directory # features/steps/demo_steps.py:53
    When I run the Caducus analyze command for group_id "hdfs-demo:DataNode"                            # features/steps/demo_steps.py:103
    Then the command succeeds                                                                           # features/steps/demo_steps.py:113
    And the radar output includes group "hdfs-demo:DataNode"                                            # features/steps/demo_steps.py:135
    And the radar output includes ranked blips with weight, temporal, and member count                  # features/steps/demo_steps.py:141

  Scenario: Unknown group returns a clear operator message                                              # features/demo_vertical_slice.feature:35
    Given a local demo dataset file with HDFS-style log rows                                            # features/steps/demo_steps.py:27
    And a Caducus data directory is configured                                                          # features/steps/demo_steps.py:47
    Given canonical event records already exist for group_id "hdfs-demo:DataNode" in the data directory # features/steps/demo_steps.py:53
    When I run the Caducus analyze command for group_id "hdfs-demo:DoesNotExist"                        # features/steps/demo_steps.py:103
    Then the command succeeds                                                                           # features/steps/demo_steps.py:113
    And the CLI says no events were found for group_id "hdfs-demo:DoesNotExist"                         # features/steps/demo_steps.py:162

  Scenario: Source column controls grouping prefix                  # features/demo_vertical_slice.feature:41
    Given a local demo dataset file with HDFS-style log rows        # features/steps/demo_steps.py:27
    And a Caducus data directory is configured                      # features/steps/demo_steps.py:47
    Given a local demo dataset file with a custom source column     # features/steps/demo_steps.py:37
    When I run the Caducus demo ingest command with that input file # features/steps/demo_steps.py:95
    And I run the Caducus groups command                            # features/steps/demo_steps.py:108
    Then the command succeeds                                       # features/steps/demo_steps.py:113
    And the CLI includes group_id "bgl-demo:KERNEL"                 # features/steps/demo_steps.py:156

2 features passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped
7 scenarios passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped
45 steps passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped
Took 0min 7.233s: 2 features, 7 scenarios, 45 steps all passing.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Updated README intro for audience clarity: added a broad opening line about teams running production systems, moved the pager line to second line, and kept the radar-value line immediately after.
